name: DOGE Range Failure Reacceptance v1
thesis: DOGE 4h is the best-fit primary lane for testing range-failure reversal after a multi-bar breakout loses acceptance and re-enters balance. The edge should come from balance reacceptance after failed extension, not from single-bar hero reversal timing.
edge_mechanism: range failure mean reversion after value reacceptance
invalidation_condition: Abort if reacceptance setups remain too rare, if excursion and reacceptance logic conflict, or if mean reversion gains do not survive costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A multi-bar breakout attempt loses acceptance, price re-enters balance, and the move back through value continues for several bars.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: When a breakout cannot sustain acceptance, trapped late entrants can fuel a reversal back into the prior range; DOGE 4h overshoots should make these failures visible.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: DOGE 4h is the best-fit primary lane because failed breakouts on a volatile 4h canvas can still generate enough excursion to matter without requiring intraday noise chasing.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If DOGE remains in one-way directional expansion rather than failed breakouts, reacceptance entries will stand in front of continuation.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If participation exists but reversals are shallow, bring the first profit target closer to balance before tightening the trigger.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; excursion and reacceptance are complementary
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by proximity thresholds instead of exact reversal-candle requirements

long: Take profit: EMA_55 or 1.2 * ATR_14, whichever comes first
short: Take profit: EMA_55 or 1.2 * ATR_14, whichever comes first
